questions.question: Q1. Under the 'Peak Grid Shaving' dispatch strategy, when does the battery begin discharging to support the loads?

answers.a: As soon as solar irradiance drops below 100 W/m²
answers.b: Only when the utility grid is completely disconnected (Islanded)
answers.c: When the power import from the grid exceeds the configured Grid Import Cap
answers.d: Whenever the battery State of Charge is above 90%

explanations.a: Irradiance levels alone do not trigger battery discharge in shaving mode; net load imports do.
explanations.b: This describes emergency outage mode, not shaving mode.
explanations.c: Correct. In shaving mode, the battery discharges to keep grid import below the specified cap.
explanations.d: The battery only discharges when a deficit occurs that exceeds the grid shaving import limit.

questions.question: Q2. How does the activation of Demand Response (DR) affect consumer load demand in this simulation?

answers.a: It completely shuts down industrial loads to protect the grid
answers.b: It sheds residential and industrial loads by 15%, and commercial loads by 10%
answers.c: It increases the charging efficiency of the lithium battery to 100%
answers.d: It locks out the wind turbine turbine rotation

explanations.a: Loads are shed percentage-wise, not completely shut down.
explanations.b: Correct. Activating the DR toggle applies a 15% reduction to residential/industrial loads and 10% to commercial loads.
explanations.c: Demand response manages consumer loads; it does not change battery electrochemical efficiency.
explanations.d: Wind turbine safety is governed by wind speed cut-out limits, not demand response.

questions.question: Q3. If the total load demand is 900 kW and total renewable generation is 1200 kW, what is the net power balance of the microgrid?

explanations.a: A negative balance represents a deficit; here we have more generation than demand.
explanations.b: Correct. Net power balance = Generation - Demand = 1200 kW - 900 kW = +300 kW.
explanations.c: This value incorrectly sums generation and demand.
explanations.d: The grid has a surplus of 300 kW, so it is not balanced at zero.

questions.question: Q4. What is the round-trip or charging efficiency rating of the battery process modeled in this Smart EMS controller?

explanations.a: No battery chemistry is 100% efficient due to thermal and chemical losses.
explanations.b: Correct. The lithium battery charging and discharging process has a 92% efficiency coefficient.
explanations.c: The battery efficiency is modeled at 92%, not 75%.
explanations.d: 55% is the efficiency rating for the hydrogen fuel cell.

questions.question: Q5. What is the maximum output limit of the hydrogen fuel cell when discharging stored hydrogen to cover power deficits?

explanations.a: The fuel cell output limit is higher than 50 kW.
explanations.b: Correct. The hydrogen fuel cell output is capped at a maximum of 150 kW.
explanations.c: The maximum fuel cell discharge limit is 150 kW, not 250 kW.

questions.question: Q6. In Grid-Tied mode under 'Max Self-Consumption', what is the priority order for routing surplus renewable power?

answers.a: Grid Export -> Battery Charging -> Hydrogen Electrolyzer
answers.b: Hydrogen Electrolyzer -> Battery Charging -> Grid Export
answers.c: Battery Charging -> Hydrogen Electrolyzer -> Grid Export
answers.d: Grid Export -> Hydrogen Electrolyzer -> Battery Charging

explanations.a: Grid export is the last resort to avoid wasting power, not the first.
explanations.b: Battery storage is more efficient (92%) than hydrogen (65% in / 55% out), so battery is charged first.
explanations.c: Correct. Surplus goes to the battery first, then the electrolyzer, and finally any leftover is exported to the grid.
explanations.d: Self-consumption minimizes grid interactions, so grid export is lowest priority.

questions.question: Q7. How is the solar cell temperature (T_cell) computed in the simulation based on solar irradiance (G) and ambient temperature (T_amb)?

explanations.a: Correct. Cell temperature rises above ambient temperature as solar irradiance heating occurs, modeled as T_amb + G * 0.025.
explanations.b: Cell temperature increases with solar irradiance, not decreases.
explanations.c: This is not the formula for solar cell thermal modeling.
explanations.d: Irradiance (G) is a key variable in determining cell heating.

questions.question: Q8. What is the total storage capacity limit of the hydrogen tank in this system?

explanations.a: The storage limit is larger than 5 kg.
explanations.b: Correct. The hydrogen tank capacity is exactly 20 kg of H2.
explanations.c: The capacity limit is 20 kg, not 100 kg.

questions.question: Q9. What is the efficiency coefficient of the hydrogen fuel cell when converting stored hydrogen back to electricity?

explanations.a: 92% is the efficiency rating of the battery, not the fuel cell.
explanations.b: Fuel cell electrical conversion efficiency is lower than 75%.
explanations.c: 65% is the input efficiency of the electrolyzer.
explanations.d: Correct. The fuel cell operates at an efficiency of 55% in converting chemical energy to electricity.

questions.question: Q10. Which scenario preset simulates a complete grid outage, forcing the microgrid into Islanded (off-grid) connection mode?

explanations.a: Standard Day is a normal grid-tied day.
explanations.b: Peak Demand tests grid shaving and shedding, but remains grid-tied.
explanations.c: Storm Event triggers turbine lockout braking due to high winds.
explanations.d: Correct. The Grid Outage preset disconnects the utility grid, forcing the EMS into emergency islanded mode.
